title: Pyrrosia boothii
intro: Pyrrosia boothii, also known as Pyrrosia, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities that can aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pyrrosia boothii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pyrrosia boothii is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as being used in topical applications, to take advantage of its health benefits.
taxonomy: Pyrrosia boothii is a species of plant in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody ferns. This plant belongs to the genus Pyrrosia, which consists of tropical and subtropical ferns. The species Pyrrosia boothii is native to Southeast Asia and is often found growing on trees or rocky outcrops. Pyrrosia boothii is a type of rhizomatous fern, meaning it has an underground stem system that produces new plants. Its classification also includes being a member of the class Polypodiopsida and the division Pteridophyta.
